game_info.title: ZigZag Infinity
game_info.concept:
Stay on the wall and do as many zigzags as you can!

App Store URL: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/zigzag/id951364656

================================================================================
Full Description:
================================================================================

Stay on the wall and do as many zigzags as you can!

Just tap the screen to change the direction of the ball. Try not to fall off the edges!

How far can you go?
game_info.description:
Welcome to ZigZag Infinity! A fast-paced isometric runner where you control a sphere rolling along a treacherous, procedurally generated wall.

Your goal is simple: stay on the wall and go as far as you can! The wall zigs and zags, and you must switch the ball's direction at the right moment to avoid falling into the abyss. Collect pink diamonds for extra points and watch out as the path crumbles behind you!

Test your reflexes and rhythm in this addictive arcade experience. How many zigzags can you master?
game_info.controls:
- SPACE, Arrow Keys, Z, or Shift: Switch direction (Zig / Zag)
- ENTER: Start game
- ESC: Pause / Resume
- R: Restart game (from Game Over screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Test the basic pathfinding and direction switching mechanism to ensure the game is playable and winnable.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The automated controller checks the tile immediately ahead in the current direction of movement. If that tile does not exist (is void), it sends an input signal to switch direction, ensuring the ball stays on the path.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The ball should traverse the path indefinitely without falling off, collecting points and demonstrating stable physics and collision detection.

TEST_2.test_description: Stress test the input handling and physics stability with random inputs.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The controller sends direction switch inputs at random intervals (roughly every 30-60 frames). This simulates erratic player behavior.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The ball will likely fall off quickly, but the game should handle the failure state correctly, transitioning to GAME_OVER and resetting properly without crashing.

TEST_3.test_description: Test the game cycle transition reliability (Start -> Play -> Over -> Start).
TEST_3.strategy_description: The controller will start the game, let it run for 2 seconds, force a restart, and repeat. This validates memory management and state reset logic.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The game should restart cleanly multiple times with no memory leaks, artifacting, or state corruption.
